prevalence.source: Pediatric respiratory surveillance; RSV season reports

burden.dalyPerCapita: moderate in infants due to hospitalization risk
burden.mortalityRate: Low; higher in premature and high-risk infants

vulnerablePopulations: infants with cardiac or pulmonary comorbidity
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis of wheezing and respiratory distress in infants; imaging only if severe or atypical

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; seasonal surveillance in pediatrics
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Hundreds of thousands of episodes annually
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with supportive care and oxygen when needed

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in pediatrics; hospital admission for hypoxia
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care, oxygen, hydration; avoid routine bronchodilators unless indicated
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Seasonal hospital crowding and limited ICU beds during peaks
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Established pediatric protocols for bronchiolitis management
